section.name: social
section.term: A feed record capturing that a member performed a verb on an object; drives the public social feed.
A derived set of members reachable through shared private community co-membership, computed via graph traversal. Not followers, friends, or a manual connection list.
A named, visibility-scoped group of members; the primary organizational unit of the social layer.
A titled, threaded group discussion anchored to a visibility scope.
Graph traversal of circle reach, built from private community co-membership, determining which members and objects are reachable at each circle depth.
A scheduled social gathering with a title, optional time bounds, visibility, and an attached conversation. Bare "event" always means a social event — never a calendar entry.
An open one-to-one member communication outside Social context; lives in a two-participant message thread owned by Messages.
An alert delivered to a specific member about something that happened and is relevant to them.
A member's real-time availability signal recording current status, availability, and runtime.
A member's social-layer identity; distinct from the API identity record.
An explicit, directional record from one member to another expressing a social stance (currently: block only).
A post added to an existing conversation or event by any member who can view it. A reply is not a direct Message.
A private one-to-one communication initiated from Social context such as a profile, conversation, community, event, reply, presence record, or activity item. Owned by Social.
The access scope of a social object determining which members can see it.

section.name: spatial
section.term: The placement mode of a world object — carry (user-anchored) or field (world-anchored).
The primary user-following region; objects anchored to a member that persist across spatial movement.
The primary world-anchored region; the shared spatial panorama where field objects are placed at coordinates. The output of the maps service is called the field — not a map.
A visual point-of-interest placed at a position in the field, authored by the maps service.
A carry-layer content surface loaded inside a surface by the runtime via content_source.url when content_source.kind is runtime_panel. Distinct from surface.

section.name: surface-system
section.term: An executable interaction exposed by an object or surface; connects user intent to service behavior.
A structured content region within a surface; organizes objects, cards, controls, and findings.
A visual presentation wrapper for an object within a block; renderer-level, temporary, replaceable. Distinct from surface.
A navigable ordered collection of surfaces within a region; represents movement through contextual space.
An attached stack region that follows the runtime viewport or user.
A spatially anchored placement for a surface.
A discovered or computed informational result surfaced by a service; distinct from notification and activity.
The currently active surface in a stack.
A thresholded transition that changes the focus surface in a stack.
The model governing spatial movement through the runtime; not document traversal.
A partially visible surface adjacent to the focus surface.
A renderable entity within a block, originating from a service (place, member, message, etc.).
A persistent spatial zone within a runtime; primary regions are carry and field.
An ordered collection of surfaces with one focus surface.
A persistent interactive container around service-backed or runtime-backed content. Distinct from panel and card.
Normalized runtime-safe content carried by a surface. A runtime renders this object and never parses its source HTML.
The server-side conversion of an external resource into a normalized Surface Object without executing source scripts.
A public HTTP or HTTPS resource discovered by Find. Its source representation is not a runtime payload.
The visible presentation region for a stack.
Reserved. An independently managed spatial container with z-order and overlap; not a current Elonn primitive. Do not use.

section.name: service-runtime
section.term: The identity and authentication authority for all Elonn services; owns members, sessions, and tokens.
A scheduled Time entry with start and end bounds, represented as a VEVENT for calendar interoperability. Distinct from a Social event.
A calendar entry in the time service; may mirror a social event. Never bare "event" when referring to a time service object.
The named, versioned, platform-neutral payload definition that tells every runtime what primitives, capabilities, actions, and objects to expect.
The search service that owns query records, findings, and heads (classifications). Distinct from discovery.
The spatial data service; authority for field layout, field width, and markers.
A person with a registered account in the Elonn identity system.
A platform adapter that consumes the World runtime contract and renders its primitives in a complete executable environment such as web, Android, iPad, XR, or desktop. Always qualify when ambiguous.
A bounded interaction context. Always qualify: auth session, runtime session, surface session, or find session. Never bare "session".
The social object authority; owns conversations, communities, events, profiles, presence, activity, notifications, relationships, circles, discovery graph behavior, and Social DMs.
An actionable Time entry with completion state and optional start, due date, recurrence, priority, and reminders, represented as a VTODO for calendar interoperability.
The calendar, scheduling, and task service; owns calendars, appointments, tasks, recurrence, reminders, schedule views, and calendar mirrors.
A credential issued by the API service after authentication; identifies the member across all service calls.
The browser adapter for the platform-neutral runtime contract and the first runtime conformance target.
The composition service that assembles identity, maps, social, messages, and time data into the platform-neutral runtime contract consumed by all runtimes.

distinction.rule: Messages are open one-to-one member threads outside Social context. Replies go into Social conversations and events.
Messages are open one-to-one member threads outside Social context. Social DMs are one-to-one private communications initiated from Social context and owned by Social.
Thread is a two-participant message or Social DM container. Conversations are group discussions.
Bare "event" means a social event (social.elonn.local). Calendar events belong to the time service.
An event is a Social gathering. An appointment is a scheduled Time entry represented as VEVENT.
Circles are derived from community membership. Relationships are explicit records (block only).
Discovery is the social graph traversal. Find is the search service.
The maps service produces the field. The output is not called a map.

A surface is a persistent contextual environment within a deck. A panel is carry-layer content loaded inside a surface via content_source.url when content_source.kind is runtime_panel. Different levels of the system.
A card is a visual wrapper for an object — renderer-level, temporary. A surface is the contextual environment the card lives inside.
A finding is a discovered or computed result delivered to a surface. A notification is a targeted alert delivered to a member.
A finding is a candidate discovered by Find. A Surface Object is normalized runtime-safe content produced and persisted by Surface.
A surface is the durable member-scoped container with placement and identity. A Surface Object is the renderable normalized content carried by that surface.
Discovery is the social graph traversal (how members are reached). Findings are the computed outputs delivered to surfaces.
Window is reserved and must not be used for any current Elonn primitive.
